title: chenopodium hircinum subf. densum
intro: Chenopodium hircinum subf. densum is a lesser-known subspecies of the Chenopodium hircinum plant, which is a type of goosefoot or lamb's-quarters plant, commonly found in dry to moist areas. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Chenopodium hircinum subf. densum include its potential to treat fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and laxative. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in folk medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
